summary:
Cleft lip and palate exhibit multifactorial inheritance involving multiple genetic loci alongside environmental influences like nutrition and teratogen exposures. The condition shows familial aggregation but does not follow simple Mendelian patterns due to its complexity.
```

The summary captures the core aspects of the inherited nature of cleft lip and palate without extraneous details while addressing both genetic and non-genetic contributing elements. ```markdown
``` Summary: Cleft lip and palate result from a combination of multiple genetic factors and environmental influences, fitting a multifactorial inheritance pattern rather than being solely determined by one gene. This explains why the disorder tends to cluster within families yet doesn't conform to straightforward single-gene transmission rules.
```
